reasoning: ADCs are controlled under 3A001.a.5 only if they exceed specific resolution/sample-rate thresholds (e.g., ≥16-bit resolution with sample rate >65 MSPS). At 24-bit/256 kSPS (0.256 MSPS) per channel, this sigma-delta ADC is far below the 3A001.a.5 control parameters and is not described elsewhere on the CCL, so it falls to EAR99.

item_name: Quad RF-DAC / quad RF-ADC mixed-signal front-end
reasoning: The device integrates 4 GSPS analog-to-digital converter channels (≥12-bit class, well above the 1.0 GSPS threshold) and 12 GSPS digital-to-analog converter channels, both of which are 'microwave/RF' data converters captured by ECCN 3A001.a.5 (ADCs) and 3A001.a.6 (DACs). The ADC parameters (multi-GSPS, ≥10-bit resolution) place the integrated circuit under 3A001.a.5.a; the embedded DUC/DDC and JESD204B/C interface do not remove it from this control. (The 12 GSPS DAC channels are independently controlled under 3A001.a.6.)

item_name: 6 GSPS RF digital-to-analog converter, to 5 GHz
reasoning: 16-bit DAC with 6 GSPS sample rate exceeds 3A001.a.5.a thresholds (resolution ≥14 bits with sample rate >3.5 GSample/s), classifying as 3A001.a.5.a.

item_name: Wideband 2x2 RF agile transceiver, 47 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: Monolithic integrated transceiver covering 47 MHz–6 GHz with instantaneous channel bandwidth up to 56 MHz and integrated ADC/DAC and synthesizers falls under 3A001.b.7 as a frequency-agile/wideband mixed-signal IC for radio communication; tuning range exceeds 500 MHz across 43.5 MHz–8.5 GHz.

A 300 MHz 32-bit fixed/floating-point audio DSP does not meet the ADP/microprocessor performance thresholds of 3A991.a or 4A003, and audio mixing/filtering/EQ is consumer-grade signal processing not controlled under 3A001.a.7 or other CCL entries. As a general-purpose audio DSP, it falls to EAR99.

item_name: Narrow-band sub-1 GHz ISM RF transceiver
reasoning: Commercial sub-1 GHz ISM-band RF transceiver providing telecommunications transmit/receive functions; not meeting 5A001 performance thresholds (no spread spectrum/frequency hopping controls, civil ISM band, standard FSK/GFSK). Classified under 5A991 as telecommunications equipment subject to EAR but not on 5A001.

item_name: Integrated dual-channel RF transceiver, 75 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: Monolithic microwave/RF integrated transceiver operating across 75 MHz-6 GHz with instantaneous bandwidth ~200 MHz falls under 3A001.b.7 as an integrated transceiver/MMIC-type RF component (frequency >2.7 GHz extending beyond 3.2 GHz). Tunable >2.7 GHz with multi-channel TX/RX capability characteristic of SDR transceivers controlled under 3A001.b.
General-purpose microprocessor/SoC not meeting 3A001.a.3 performance thresholds; falls under 3A991.a.1 as a microprocessor microcircuit subject to AT controls.

Item is an information security commodity using cryptography for confidentiality with symmetric (AES) and asymmetric (ECDSA/ECDH) algorithms exceeding the cryptographic note thresholds, classified under Category 5 Part 2 as 5A002.a (cryptographic information security).

item_name: 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi + Bluetooth module, 4 MB flash
reasoning: Wi-Fi/Bluetooth radio module is a 'telecommunications' item employing digital modulation that meets the definition of information security in 5A002, but as a mass-market wireless module operating in commonly available bands it is classified under 5A992.c as a mass-market commodity.

FPGAs are integrated circuits subject to the EAR; a low-cost, low-density (~3,520 logic cells) commercial FPGA does not meet the technical thresholds of 3A001.a.7 (programmable logic devices), so it falls under 3A991 as a general electronic component not controlled for national security.

Hardware secure element implementing ECDSA/ECDH (asymmetric >112-bit) and AES-128 (symmetric >56-bit) cryptography for authentication and secure boot is an 'information security' item designed to use cryptography, classified under Category 5 Part 2, 5A002.a.

General-purpose 16-bit microcontroller/DSC running at ~60 MIPS is well below the performance thresholds for controlled microprocessors/DSPs in 3A001.a.3 (which targets high-performance/adjusted peak performance devices). No specialized cryptographic, radiation-hardened, or other CCL-controlled features are described, so the part falls outside CCL coverage and is EAR99.

A basic Layer-2 managed 10/100 Mbps Ethernet switch IC does not meet the parameters of 5A991 (which historically covered higher-speed/telecom switching) or 4A994, and contains no controlled cryptographic functionality under 5A002. Such commodity networking switch ICs are typically classified EAR99.

Standard commercial 10/100 Ethernet PHY transceivers operating at 100 Mbit/s are not described by any Category 5 Part 1 telecommunications control (well below the data-rate and other parameters of 5A001) and contain no controlled cryptographic functionality (not 5A002). As a mass-market commercial networking IC subject to the EAR but not listed on the CCL, it falls under EAR99.

Standard 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PHY transceivers are commercial off-the-shelf networking components that do not meet the technical thresholds of Category 5 Part 1 telecommunications controls (e.g., 5A991) and are not described elsewhere on the CCL, so they default to EAR99.

MCU incorporates AES/3DES cryptographic functionality classifiable under Cat 5 Part 2. As a mass-market component with standard symmetric crypto (AES) it falls under 5A992.c as information security item not controlled under 5A002.

Short-range Bluetooth Low Energy transceiver module operating in the 2.4 GHz ISM band is a commercial wireless 'information security' / telecommunications item. As a mass-market short-range wireless device employing standard Bluetooth, it falls under Category 5 Part 2 as 5A992.c (mass market encryption commodity).

item_name: Quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 compute module (2 GB / 16 GB)
reasoning: General-purpose digital computer module not meeting 4A003 APP thresholds; quad-core Cortex-A72 at 1.5 GHz falls well below high-performance computing limits, so it classifies under the catch-all computer entry 4A994.

General-purpose 32-bit microcontroller (ARM Cortex-M4 at 168 MHz) does not meet the performance thresholds of 3A001.a.3 (microprocessors/microcomputers) which focus on specialized high-performance or radiation-hardened devices. Standard commercial MCUs of this class are typically EAR99.

A standard 1-inch broadband dielectric mirror for the visible band (400-750 nm) on a fused-silica substrate does not meet the technical parameters of ECCN 6A004 (which controls mirrors with active deformable elements, specific high-energy laser damage thresholds, or specialized substrates with controlled thermal properties). It is not coated for controlled IR/laser bands under 6A005 optics notes. As a commercial off-the-shelf visible mirror, it is subject to the EAR but not listed on the CCL, hence EAR99.

A passive mechanical kinematic mount for holding/aligning 1-inch optics has no controlled optical, electronic, or precision-metrology parameters under Category 6 (optics) or Category 2 (machine tools/precision). Such opto-mechanical hardware is not described on the CCL and is treated as EAR99.

A single-core 1 GHz ARM Cortex-A8 general-purpose applications microprocessor does not meet the performance thresholds (APP) or specialized characteristics of 3A001.a.3 (microprocessors) and is not otherwise described on the CCL. Such commodity-grade embedded processors are typically classified EAR99.
